The successful candidate will work closely with engineering, procurement, operations, and project teams to develop commercially viable bids while ensuring compliance with technical, quality, safety, and client requirements.</p><p><br></p><p>Key Responsibilities</p><ul><li>Review and analyze tender documents, technical specifications, and engineering drawings.</li><li>Prepare detailed cost estimates for labor, materials, equipment, and subcontractor services.</li><li>Conduct quantity take-offs for piping, structural, mechanical, and civil scopes.</li><li>Participate in site visits, pre-bid meetings, and client clarification sessions.</li><li>Obtain and evaluate vendor and subcontractor quotations.</li><li>Support project scheduling, resource planning, and execution strategies.</li><li>Contribute to bid reviews, risk assessments, and proposal development.</li><li>Maintain estimating databases, historical cost records, and benchmarking tools.</li></ul><p><br></p><p>Requirements</p><ul><li>Degree or Diploma in Mechanical Engineering, Civil/Structural Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Quantity Surveying, or a related discipline.</li><li>Minimum 10 years of experience in estimating for EPC projects, industrial construction, oil &amp;
